non_negotiables: Mutual indemnification is the minimum acceptable structure — unilateral indemnification in vendor's favor is non-negotiable.
Cap on indemnification liability must mirror the overall liability cap.
IP indemnification from vendor for third-party infringement claims is always required.

standard_positions.title: Mutual indemnification scope
standard_positions.our_position: Each party indemnifies the other for its own negligence, willful misconduct, and breach. Broad 'hold harmless' language must be limited to actual claims, not theoretical exposure.
acceptable_variations: Carve-out for gross negligence where both parties share fault proportionally
IP indemnification handled in a separate IP clause
standard_positions.rationale: Unlimited mutual indemnification creates unquantifiable exposure. Must be scoped.

red_flags: Unilateral indemnification obligation on Ironbridge without reciprocal protection
'Consequential damages' included in indemnification scope without cap
No carve-out for gross negligence or willful misconduct of the indemnified party
sections.industry_baseline: Industry standard is mutual indemnification with carve-outs for gross negligence. Engineering sector commonly adds professional liability carve-outs.

non_negotiables: Total liability cap must be expressed as a fixed multiple of fees paid — minimum 12 months of fees.
Consequential, indirect, and punitive damages must be mutually excluded.
Death/personal injury and fraud carve-outs from the liability cap are always accepted.

standard_positions.title: Liability cap multiple
standard_positions.our_position: Cap at 100% of fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim. Will negotiate to 200% for high-risk engagements.
acceptable_variations: Higher cap for data breach scenarios (up to 200% of annual fees)
Separate cap for IP infringement claims
standard_positions.rationale: Proportionate to contract value — protects both parties from catastrophic loss.

red_flags: Uncapped liability for any category of loss
Asymmetric cap where our exposure is higher than counterparty's
Exclusion of consequential damages waived for specific breach types without a corresponding cap
sections.industry_baseline: Mutual exclusion of consequential damages and cap at 12 months of fees is standard across engineering services contracts.

non_negotiables: Governing law must be a common law jurisdiction — no civil law jurisdictions without legal review.
New York or Delaware preferred. Ontario, England & Wales acceptable.
Exclusive jurisdiction clause must be mutual — no unilateral venue selection.

standard_positions.title: Preferred jurisdiction
standard_positions.our_position: New York law, exclusive jurisdiction of courts in New York County.
acceptable_variations: Delaware for US counterparties incorporated there
standard_positions.rationale: New York has the most developed commercial contract law in common law jurisdictions.

red_flags: Jurisdiction in a country with limited enforcement of foreign judgments
Dispute resolution requiring travel to counterparty's home jurisdiction exclusively
Arbitration clause that waives right to court proceedings for injunctive relief
sections.industry_baseline: New York and Delaware are the most common governing law choices for US engineering services contracts.

non_negotiables: Minimum 30-day written cure period before termination for cause.
Payment for all services rendered through termination date, regardless of which party terminates.
Survival clause must cover: indemnification, confidentiality, IP ownership, limitation of liability, and governing law.

standard_positions.title: Termination for convenience
standard_positions.our_position: Either party may terminate for convenience on 60 days notice. Client pays for all work completed plus reasonable wind-down costs (capped at 30 days of fees).
acceptable_variations: 30-day notice for contracts under $50K total value
standard_positions.rationale: Long-running engineering engagements require adequate wind-down to protect both parties.

red_flags: Client can terminate for convenience with less than 30 days notice on a long-term engagement
No payment obligation for completed work upon termination for convenience
Survival clause that does not include confidentiality or IP ownership
sections.industry_baseline: 30-60 day termination for convenience notice periods are standard. Survival of key provisions is universal.

non_negotiables: Ironbridge retains all IP in pre-existing tools, methodologies, and frameworks (background IP).
Client owns deliverables specifically created for them (foreground IP) upon full payment.
License to background IP embedded in deliverables is limited, non-exclusive, and non-transferable.

standard_positions.title: Background IP license
standard_positions.our_position: Client receives a perpetual, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use background IP embedded in deliverables, solely for their internal business purposes.
acceptable_variations: Sublicensing to affiliates permitted with prior written consent
Source code escrow for software deliverables
standard_positions.rationale: Background IP is core to our business model. Assigning it would destroy the value of our methodology.

red_flags: Work-for-hire provisions that assign all IP including background IP to client
IP assignment without requiring full payment as a condition precedent
Broad IP license that includes the right to sublicense to competitors
sections.industry_baseline: Background/foreground IP split is the engineering industry standard. Full work-for-hire is uncommon except in bespoke software development.

non_negotiables: Mutual confidentiality — both parties' information protected equally.
Standard carve-outs: publicly available information, independently developed, legally required disclosure.

standard_positions.title: Confidentiality term
standard_positions.our_position: 5 years post-termination for standard confidential information. Perpetual for trade secrets.
acceptable_variations: Perpetual only for specifically marked trade secret materials
standard_positions.rationale: Engineering methodologies have long competitive half-lives. 5 years provides adequate protection.

red_flags: Unilateral confidentiality protecting only client's information
Confidentiality term of less than 2 years
No carve-out for legally required disclosure (court orders, regulatory requests)
sections.industry_baseline: Mutual NDA with 3-5 year post-term obligation is universal in engineering services. Perpetual for trade secrets.
